hero_subtitle: SFTY links corporate adverse media screenings with provider outputs and case workflows to preserve audit trails and streamline AML compliance operations for digital asset firms.

problem_title: Adverse media reviews depend on manual workflows
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to consolidate adverse media screening results from multiple providers into a clear and auditable case record. This fragmentation delays risk assessment for corporate clients and weakens traceability for ongoing AML operations and reporting.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects adverse media screening outputs, regulatory rules, case evidence and reviewer actions into a unified workflow to help compliance teams manage corporate risk with clear audit trails and consistent reporting.
workflow_title: From adverse media alerts to cases
workflow_description: SFTY processes adverse media screening results by routing alerts into structured cases with evidence and decision logs for thorough compliance review and reporting.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented checks. One audit-ready layer
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects adverse media outputs into structured reviews, cases, decisions and audit evidence, enabling compliance teams to manage corporate risk with clearer operational control.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY provides VASP compliance teams with a controlled layer that integrates provider outputs and preserves audit-ready evidence for enterprise-grade operational trust.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance

ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for adverse media compliance?
faq_1_answer: SFTY connects adverse media screening outputs into structured workflows, case management, regulatory rule monitoring and reporting for digital asset compliance teams reviewing corporate clients.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle corporate adverse media cases?
faq_2_answer: SFTY routes adverse media alerts into review workflows, opens cases for risk investigation, records decisions and links evidence to build a clear audit trail for corporate compliance.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case history, decision logs and supporting documents with traceable evidence packages to support audit readiness and regulatory inspections.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing compliance providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY integrates seamlessly with sanctions, PEP and adverse media providers to connect their results within one operational compliance layer.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace sanctions or PEP screening tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY does not replace screening tools but connects their outputs, turning raw alerts into structured reviews, decisions, cases and regulatory reports.
final_title: Structure corporate adverse media reviews with SFTY
final_description: Review how SFTY connects adverse media screening outputs into case workflows with audit-ready evidence and operational compliance controls.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
